agents.backstory_hint: Analytical engineer with a strong grasp of system architecture and incident prioritization.
agents.goal: Analyze bug reports to determine severity and identify the relevant service module.

agents.backstory_hint: Organized administrative assistant who keeps the backlog clean and action-oriented.
agents.goal: File bug reports into the tracking system with proper labeling and owner assignment.

ambiguities: What is the specific tool used for issue tracking (Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues) so the correct API can be used?
What is the specific communication channel (e.g., Slack, Email, PagerDuty) used for the human checkpoint notification?
assumptions: The tracking system is a generic issue tracker (e.g., Jira, Linear, or GitHub Issues).
Non-P0 issues bypass the human checkpoint and proceed directly to filing.
The 'Service-to-Team' map is a static data structure accessible to the triage agent.

tasks.description: Examine the incoming bug report for severity (e.g., P0 vs non-critical) and map the issue to a service/team based on the description or stack trace.
tasks.expected_output: A severity assessment, a target service module, and an estimated owner for the issue.

tasks.description: Review the high-severity P0 classification to confirm if it warrants immediate paging/escalation.
tasks.expected_output: Confirmation of P0 status or a downgrade to a lower priority.

tasks.description: Create an issue in the tracking system with the severity label and suggested owner assigned.
tasks.expected_output: A successfully created issue record in the bug tracking system.

tools.inputs: severity label, owner, description, stack trace
tools.name: Bug Tracker Connector
tools.purpose: File bug reports with appropriate metadata.

tools.name: Service-to-Team Mapper
tools.purpose: Identify the responsible team based on service or component tags.

workflow.description: Standardize bug intake by analyzing severity and service area, then routing them to the appropriate team or triggering a human review for critical outages.
workflow.name: Bug Triage and Routing
workflow.trigger: A new entry in the shared intake form containing a bug report, Sentry alert, or support ticket.
